title: Visiting the Doctor
conversation: Doctor: Hi, I’m Dr. Wilson. What seems to be the trouble? Grace: I’ve been under the weather for days. Doctor: The flu is going around. Did you get the vaccine? Grace: No, this year I didn’t get around to it. Doctor: What are your symptoms? Grace: I’ve had a splitting headache since yesterday morning. My whole body hurts. Doctor: You have all the telltale signs of the flu. Let me take your temperature . . . 101. That’s on the high side. Grace: My throat hurts, too. Doctor: Let me take a peek. Yes, your throat is very red. It looks like a garden-variety flu. Grace: Are you going to prescribe some medicine for it? Doctor: You can take Tylenol for your headache. Also, be sure to get plenty of rest and drink lots of water. Grace: How long do you think this is going to drag on? Doctor: You should be back on your feet in a week or so. Grace: I have to get better quickly. I’m needed at the office. Doctor: Well, there’s no magic bullet. You’re going to have to let this run its course.

idioms.idiom: What seems to be the trouble?
idioms.meaning_en: a phrase used to ask what is wrong or what medical problem someone has
idioms.meaning_vi: Có chuyện gì vậy? (câu hỏi về tình trạng bệnh lý)
idioms.example: The doctor entered the room and asked, 'What seems to be the trouble?'
idioms.context: Doctor: Hi, I’m Dr. Wilson. What seems to be the trouble?

idioms.meaning_vi: cảm thấy không khỏe, bị ốm
idioms.example: I'm feeling a bit under the weather, so I'm going to stay home today.
idioms.context: Grace: I’ve been under the weather for days.

idioms.meaning_en: spreading from person to person (usually referring to an illness)
idioms.meaning_vi: đang lây lan, đang có dịch
idioms.example: There is a nasty stomach bug going around the school.
idioms.context: Doctor: The flu is going around.

idioms.meaning_en: to find the time to do something that has been intended for a while
idioms.meaning_vi: có thời gian/thu xếp được thời gian để làm việc gì đó
idioms.example: I meant to fix the sink, but I never got around to it.
idioms.context: Grace: No, this year I didn’t get around to it.

idioms.meaning_en: a very severe or intense headache
idioms.example: Turn down the music; I have a splitting headache.
idioms.context: Grace: I’ve had a splitting headache since yesterday morning.

idioms.meaning_en: obvious signs that reveal the truth about a situation
idioms.meaning_vi: dấu hiệu rõ rệt, dấu hiệu cho biết điều gì đó
idioms.example: Weight loss and fatigue are telltale signs of the illness.
idioms.context: Doctor: You have all the telltale signs of the flu.

idioms.meaning_en: slightly higher than what is considered normal or average
idioms.meaning_vi: hơi cao so với mức bình thường
idioms.example: The price for the car is a bit on the high side.

idioms.meaning_en: to look at something quickly or secretly
idioms.example: Let me take a peek at your notes before the exam starts.
idioms.context: Doctor: Let me take a peek.

idioms.meaning_vi: loại thường, không có gì đặc biệt
idioms.example: It wasn't a rare disease, just a garden-variety cold.
idioms.context: It looks like a garden-variety flu.

idioms.meaning_en: to continue for a long time in a boring or unpleasant way
idioms.meaning_vi: kéo dài lê thê, kéo dài không dứt
idioms.example: The meeting dragged on for three hours.
idioms.context: Grace: How long do you think this is going to drag on?

idioms.meaning_en: recovered from an illness or problem and able to live normally again
idioms.meaning_vi: hồi phục, khỏe lại (sau khi ốm)
idioms.example: We hope to see you back on your feet soon.
idioms.context: Doctor: You should be back on your feet in a week or so.

idioms.meaning_en: no simple solution or quick cure for a difficult problem
idioms.meaning_vi: không có phương thuốc thần kì/giải pháp tức thời
idioms.example: There is no magic bullet for weight loss; it takes exercise and diet.
idioms.context: Doctor: Well, there’s no magic bullet.

idioms.meaning_vi: phát triển và kết thúc một cách tự nhiên (để bệnh tự khỏi)
idioms.example: There's no medicine for this virus; you just have to let it run its course.
idioms.context: You’re going to have to let this run its course.
